title: JWT Beginner Guide — Start Here
seoTitle: JWT Beginner Guide — Learn JSON Web Tokens from Scratch
description: Complete JWT beginner guide. Learn token structure, claims, signing, verification, and your first JWT implementation.
keywords: jwt beginner guide,learn jwt,what is jwt token,jwt tutorial
author: JWTValidator.org

faq.q: Do I need to understand cryptography?
faq.a: Basic understanding helps, but libraries handle signing. Focus on claims, expiration, and secure storage.

faq.q: How long does it take to learn JWT?
faq.a: You can decode and understand tokens in 30 minutes. Production implementation takes longer due to security considerations.

content:
Introduction

JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) are the standard for stateless authentication in modern APIs. This guide takes you from zero to confidently working with JWTs.

Step 1: Decode a Token

Paste any JWT into our JWT Decoder. You'll see three parts: header, payload, signature.

Step 2: Understand Claims

Registered claims: iss, sub, aud, exp, nbf, iat, jti. Custom claims carry app-specific data.

Step 3: Create Your First Token

// Node.js
const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
const token = jwt.sign(
  { sub: 'user-42', role: 'user' },
  process.env.JWT_SECRET,
  { expiresIn: '1h', issuer: 'my-app' }
);
# Python
import jwt
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
token = jwt.encode(
    {'sub': 'user-42', 'exp': datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=1)},
    secret, algorithm='HS256'
)

Step 4: Verify on Every Request

Never trust decoded payload without signature verification. Always verify with the correct key and algorithm.

Next Steps

Continue with our JWT Learning Path or read JWT Authentication Explained.

title: JWT Security Best Practices
seoTitle: JWT Security Best Practices — Protect Your Tokens
description: Essential JWT security best practices: algorithm choice, secret management, storage, expiration, and attack prevention.
keywords: jwt security best practices,jwt security,jwt vulnerabilities
author: JWTValidator.org

faq.a: JWTs are secure when implemented correctly: strong keys, proper validation, short expiration, and secure storage.
Attackers set alg to 'none' hoping servers skip verification. Always whitelist allowed algorithms.

content:
Algorithm Security

  • Never accept alg: none
  • Whitelist allowed algorithms server-side
  • Prefer RS256/ES256 for public APIs
  • Use 256+ bit secrets for HS256

Storage

  • Avoid localStorage (XSS vulnerable)
  • Use httpOnly, Secure, SameSite cookies for web apps
  • Store in memory for SPAs when possible

Validation Checklist

  1. Verify signature with correct key
  2. Validate exp, nbf, iat
  3. Check iss and aud match expected values
  4. Reject unexpected algorithms

Real-World Attack Scenarios

Token Theft via XSS

Malicious scripts read tokens from localStorage. Mitigation: httpOnly cookies + CSP headers.

Algorithm Confusion

RS256 token verified with HS256 using public key as secret. Mitigation: strict algorithm whitelist.

Weak Secrets

Brute-force HS256 secrets. Mitigation: 256-bit random secrets, rate limiting.

title: OAuth 2.0 vs JWT — What's the Difference?
seoTitle: OAuth vs JWT — Understanding the Relationship
description: OAuth 2.0 vs JWT explained. OAuth is an authorization framework; JWT is a token format. Learn how they work together.
keywords: oauth vs jwt,oauth jwt difference,oauth 2.0 jwt
author: JWTValidator.org

faq.q: Is OAuth the same as JWT?
faq.a: No. OAuth 2.0 is an authorization framework. JWT is a token format. OAuth access tokens are often JWTs but can be opaque strings.

faq.q: Do I need OAuth if I use JWT?
faq.a: JWT is often used inside OAuth flows. You can use JWT without OAuth for simple API auth.

content:
OAuth 2.0

An authorization framework defining how apps obtain limited access to user resources. Roles: resource owner, client, authorization server, resource server.

JWT

A compact, self-contained token format (RFC 7519). Contains claims and a signature. Used as access tokens, ID tokens, and session tokens.

How They Work Together

  1. User authorizes via OAuth flow
  2. Authorization server returns JWT access token
  3. Client sends JWT to resource server
  4. Resource server validates JWT signature and claims

Inspect OAuth tokens with our OAuth Token Inspector.

title: Refresh Token Rotation — Secure Implementation
seoTitle: Refresh Token Rotation — JWT Security Pattern
description: Implement refresh token rotation for JWT authentication. Prevent token reuse attacks and manage session lifecycle securely.
keywords: refresh token rotation,jwt refresh token,token rotation security
author: JWTValidator.org

faq.a: Each time a refresh token is used, a new refresh token is issued and the old one is invalidated.
If a refresh token is stolen, rotation limits the attack window and enables reuse detection.

content:
Why Rotate?

Long-lived refresh tokens are high-value targets. Rotation ensures stolen tokens have a short useful life.

Implementation Pattern

// Server: on refresh
const newRefresh = generateRefreshToken(userId);
await revokeRefreshToken(oldRefreshTokenId);
await storeRefreshToken(newRefresh, userId);
return { access_token, refresh_token: newRefresh };

Reuse Detection

If a revoked refresh token is presented, invalidate all sessions for that user — possible token theft.

Check Expiration

Use our JWT Expiry Checker to monitor access token lifetimes.
